title: Write 'How to Add a New Database Mode' contributor guide
description: No guide exists for adding a new mode (like MVCC, WAL, Buffer Pool) to the Database module. Contributors must reverse-engineer the pattern from the 4289-line monolith. Write: (1) Prerequisites, (2) Files to create (engine + tests), (3) Files to modify in DatabaseModule.tsx (7 places, with line numbers), (4) Engine pattern template (annotated BTreeViz-style class), (5) UI integration steps, (6) Quality checklist, (7) Common mistakes. Save to docs/guides/adding-a-database-mode.md. This unblocks ALL future DB content additions.
acceptanceCriteria: Guide exists at docs/guides/adding-a-database-mode.md
Lists ALL files that need to be created and modified with line numbers
Includes annotated engine class template showing the step-production pattern
Includes quality checklist with at least 12 items
A developer unfamiliar with the codebase can add a mode following only this guide
Estimated time savings: 4-8 hours per new contributor

title: Write Database module architecture overview with data flow diagram
description: No architecture documentation for the DB module. Contributors can't understand the data pipeline: input → engine → steps → canvas. Write: (1) Module overview (what it does, 7 modes), (2) File map (which file does what), (3) Data flow diagram: user input → engine class → Step[] → useState → Canvas component → SVG, (4) State management pattern (useState + useRef for engines), (5) How the Learn panel sub-components connect. Use Mermaid diagrams. Save to docs/architecture/database-module.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Architecture doc exists at docs/architecture/database-module.md
Includes Mermaid diagram showing input → engine → steps → canvas data flow
Explains the useState + useRef pattern for engine state management
Maps all 24 files to their roles (engine, canvas, sidebar, etc.)
Explains the Learn panel lazy-loading architecture
A developer can understand the module's architecture in under 15 minutes by reading this doc

title: Add JSDoc to all exported functions and classes in src/lib/database/
description: Engine files have file-level JSDoc headers but function-level documentation is sparse. computeClosure, findCandidateKeys, BTreeViz.insert, HashIndexViz.search, etc. lack @param and @returns documentation. A contributor reading the code can't tell what parameters are expected or what the return value means without reading the implementation. Add JSDoc with @param, @returns, @example for every exported function and public class method.
acceptanceCriteria: Every exported function in src/lib/database/*.ts has JSDoc with @param and @returns
Every public method in BTreeViz, HashIndexViz, LSMTreeViz has JSDoc
At least 5 functions have @example showing usage
JSDoc descriptions explain WHAT the function does, not just restating the name
IDE hover shows useful documentation for all exports

title: Create DB mode scaffolding script: pnpm scaffold:db-mode
description: Adding a new DB mode requires creating 2 files and modifying 1, with zero automation. Create a scaffolding script that: (1) Creates engine file from template (class with getState/reset/operation→Step[] pattern), (2) Creates test file with 3 starter tests, (3) Updates barrel export in index.ts, (4) Prints next steps with file paths and line numbers. Script at scripts/scaffold-db-mode.ts, runnable via package.json script.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm scaffold:db-mode --name mvcc creates src/lib/database/mvcc-viz.ts from template
Template includes: class skeleton, Step interface, getState(), reset(), one sample operation
Creates test file with 3 starter tests (normal, edge, step-count)
Updates src/lib/database/index.ts with new exports
Prints clear next-steps checklist in terminal
Validates: name doesn't conflict with existing engines

title: Add type-check npm script and run in CI
description: No 'type-check' script exists in package.json. A contributor can introduce TypeScript errors that aren't caught until deployment. Add: (1) 'type-check': 'tsc --noEmit' to package.json scripts, (2) Run as part of CI pipeline (if exists), (3) Document in CONTRIBUTING.md: 'Run pnpm type-check before submitting PR'. This catches type errors early and builds confidence in changes.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json has 'type-check': 'tsc --noEmit' script
pnpm type-check runs without errors on current codebase
CI pipeline (if exists) runs type-check on PRs
CONTRIBUTING.md mentions type-check as pre-PR requirement

title: Create PR review checklist template for Database module contributions
description: No review checklist for DB module PRs. Reviewers don't know what to check. Create a .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/database-mode.md (or docs/checklists/database-mode-pr.md) with: Engine quality (step descriptions, edge cases, tests), UI integration (all 7 places updated), Content quality (problem-first description, real-world connection), Visual quality (no hardcoded colors, themed). Based on findings from all 8 audits.
acceptanceCriteria: PR checklist template exists with at least 15 items across 4 categories
Categories: Engine Quality, UI Integration, Content Quality, Testing
Each item is specific and verifiable (not vague 'looks good')
Can be copy-pasted into a PR description as a markdown checklist

title: Remove orphaned React Flow canvas node/edge components from database directory
description: src/components/canvas/nodes/database/ has EntityNode.tsx (307 lines), WeakEntityNode.tsx (245 lines), RelationshipDiamond.tsx (213 lines) and src/components/canvas/edges/database/ has CrowsFootEdge.tsx (225 lines) — 790 lines of well-built React Flow components that are COMPLETELY UNUSED by the Database module. The module uses a hand-rolled SVG canvas instead. These orphaned files confuse new contributors who think they're part of the active codebase. Either: (a) integrate them into the ER diagram mode (replacing the custom SVG), or (b) move to an 'unused' directory with a README explaining they're for future use.
acceptanceCriteria: Orphaned components are either integrated into the ER diagram mode OR moved to a clearly labeled location
If moved: README.md in the directory explains their status and future plans
If integrated: ER diagram mode uses React Flow with EntityNode/WeakEntityNode/RelationshipDiamond
No contributor confusion about whether these files are active code
